fix: upsert games/sessions on PUT to enable API sync (#50)

* fix: upsert games/sessions on PUT and add push logging

Root cause: games were created in localStorage but never POSTed
to the API. The PUT handler then returned 404 (game not found
during version check), silently preventing all subsequent syncs.

Fix: PUT handlers now create the resource if it doesn't exist
(upsert). Version check only applies when the resource already
exists on the server. Returns 201 for creates, 200 for updates.

Also adds console.warn logging to pushRequest so failed pushes
are visible in browser devtools.

feat: implement adoclint CLI tool for Antora AsciiDoc linting

Implements the complete adoclint tool from scratch:

- cmd/adoclint/main.go: Cobra CLI with 'scan' subcommand supporting
  --format (text/json/sarif), --fail-on, --verbose, --exclude, --include
- internal/model/types.go: Severity, Family, RefType, Resource,
  Reference, Diagnostic types

- internal/index/index.go: In-memory resource index with logical ID,
  abs path, and case-insensitive lookups

  xref, include, image references; skips comment lines and content
  inside delimited blocks (----, ...., ```)
- internal/resolve/resolver.go: Resolves references against the index
  supporting full Antora resource ID syntax (version\@component:module:family$path)
- internal/rules/rules.go: Converts resolve results to Diagnostics with
  broken-xref, broken-include, broken-image, broken-attachment,
  case-mismatch, and unresolved-attribute rule IDs
- internal/report/report.go: Outputs diagnostics as text, JSON, or SARIF 2.1.0
- testdata/fixtures/simple/: Sample Antora component for manual testing
- Unit tests for scan, rules, and index packages

commit.message:
fix(frontend): boot sequence race — buffer output from terminal mount

bootingRef starts true (not false) so shell output is buffered
from the moment the terminal mounts. The boot effect then either
plays the boot sequence (keeps buffering, flushes after) or
releases the hold immediately if boot is off/skipped/no bundle.

Previously bootingRef was false at mount → shell output could
write to terminal before the boot effect ran → boot text typed
over the shell prompt.

Add DISCORD_ALLOWED_USERS allowlist to restrict bot access

Only Discord user IDs listed in DISCORD_ALLOWED_USERS can interact with
the bot. Unauthorized slash commands get an ephemeral error; unauthorized
messages are silently ignored. Empty/unset = allow all (backward compat).

Env vars (allowedUsers, freeChannels) moved inside createBot() to avoid
ESM module-evaluation timing bug where top-level code runs before
dotenv.config() in index.ts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: shorter IDs, startup API fetch, and tokens guard (#45)

* fix: shorter IDs, startup API fetch, and tokens guard

Three fixes for cross-device sync usability:

1. IDs: Changed from long base-36 strings to 8-char uppercase
   alphanumeric codes (e.g., '7K3X4MBN') using an unambiguous
   charset (no 0/O/1/I). Uses timestamp+counter+random for
   collision resistance.

2. Startup sync: SessionContext now fetches all sessions from
   the API on mount and merges them with localStorage state
   (higher version wins). New MERGE_REMOTE_SESSIONS reducer
   action. GameViewPage falls back to API fetch when game is
   not in localStorage.

3. Tokens guard: NightFlashcard uses nullish coalescing on
   playerSeat.tokens to prevent TypeError for older game data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update coverage report

* fix: enable cross-device LAN access for API and UI

Two changes to make phone-to-laptop sync work over LAN:

commit.message:
Streaming render migration and buffer elimination

- Convert all render functions to write directly to out: TextIO
  instead of returning strings; use sep='' forward-pass pattern
  to handle separators without lookahead or wrappers.

- Eliminate lines lists in diagram body builders and tree renderers:
  _render_markdown_node, _render_html_node, _write_ltac_node_normalized,
  render_info all write directly to out using a first=[True] mutable flag.
  _sacm_collect_edges and _gsn_collect_edges now take a write_edge
  callback instead of an edge_lines list. _sacm_diagram_body uses two
  DFS passes (collect dot decls, then write edges); _gsn_diagram_body
  writes BottomPadding after edges (cosmetic ordering change in Mermaid
  source, golden files updated).

- Replace io.StringIO whole-document buffer in _inline_rewrite_file with
  direct streaming to a temp file. Line-ending detection reads only the
  first 4 KB. No content comparison after writing (mtime may change).

- Replace two-pass --fixmissing algorithm with a single streaming pass:
  _collect_document_element_ids pre-scans existing element IDs; smart
  placement (_emit_stubs_after, _emit_all_remaining) inserts missing
  stubs inline relative to their LTAC predecessors without buffering.
  Removes _scan_region_ends, _find_epilogue_line, _insert_missing_stubs.

- Add docs/streaming.md documenting the architecture, separator pattern,
  and all changed function signatures.

- Update AGENTS.md with streaming API conventions.

commit.message:
Adjust memory utilization thresholds to reduce false ALARM failures (#22764)

- Increase swss increase_threshold from 3% to 5% (heavy route operations
  like BGP update replication and link flap naturally cause 3-5% increase)
- Increase swss high_threshold from 8% to 10%
- Increase syncd increase_threshold from 5% to 7%
- Increase syncd high_threshold from 18% to 22% (TH2 platforms baseline
  is 9-10%, can reach 15-20% during heavy ASIC operations)
- Add Arista-7260CX3 hwsku group with higher syncd thresholds (8%/25%)
  since these TH2 broadcom platforms consistently show higher syncd memory

These thresholds caused 409 false failures across 49 test cases, 32 modules,
63 testbeds, and 17 HwSKUs in 30 days on 202511 branch.

Microsoft ADO: 37010532

[qos] Fix broken S6100 T1 headroom pool skip condition (#22909)

PR #14912 incorrectly merged separate OR conditions into a single AND
condition, creating a logical contradiction:
  hwsku in ['Force10-S6100'] AND hwsku not in [..., 'Force10-S6100', ...]
This made the skip always FALSE, causing S6100 T1 headroom pool tests
to run and fail consistently since Oct 2024.

Restore the original separate OR conditions from PR #12266.

Fixes https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/issues/12292
